title: Enter date versus scrolling to find date on date picker
excerpt: It would be great to have the ability to manually enter a date in the date picker column versus having to scroll through the months to find the date needed. At times I am planning 6-12 months in advance, more more, and i…

posts.author: katcamb (Katie Whitaker)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be great to have the ability to manually enter a date in the date picker column versus having to scroll through the months to find the date needed. At times I am planning 6-12 months in advance, more more, and it is a pain to have to do it for multiple pulses at a time. If I could manually enter the date like most form pages allow, then it would save me time. Please add this feature.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: 20 posts were merged into an existing topic: Allow Typing of Date in Date Column

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone, Thank you for taking the time to share your suggestion. Great minds think alike – our development team is already working on the capability to manually enter the date on the date picker.

Requests like yours help guide us in prioritizing the features that our customers care about the most. So keep the ideas coming because your feedback impacts tens of thousands monday.com customers who will enjoy entering dates on the date picker because of you.
﻿
Have another idea? Create a new topic to submit!

posts.author: katcamb (Katie Whitaker)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be great to have the ability to manually enter a date in the date picker column versus having to scroll through the months to find the date needed. At times I am planning 6-12 months in advance, more more, and it is a pain to have to do it for multiple pulses at a time. If I could manually enter the date like most form pages allow, then it would save me time. Please add this feature.

posts.author: GoldNetAU (Shaun Morgan)
posts.cookedHtml: I want this too. We use monday to track our supplier orders. Typically the info we have is x amount of working days from submission of order. It be great to be able to enter x days and have it find the end date by itself. Also be able to toggle to ‘exclude weekends’.

posts.author: lisamariem001 (Lisa Atkinson)
posts.cookedHtml: This is so important because it is difficult to enter future dates in a way that does not take a huge amount of time per date entry.

posts.author: Phill (Phill Allen)
posts.cookedHtml: I and my team would also like the ability to directly input the date.

posts.author: horton (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml: It is so much slower to have to scroll to dates that are far away. Please add this!

posts.author: AnderTran (Mike Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think they do this, to ensure the date gets entered in properly, without having to provide any data validation. (in case someone enters an illegal character)

But yes, I agree with you - entering a date directly would be nice.

posts.author: brooks (Brooks Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be great. Especially with timeline columns!

posts.author: Renarde (April Rooker)
posts.cookedHtml:
We create all of our new projects from a template that has a Jan. 1 start date. It’s a real hassle to have to use the current date picker to update a project’s worth of dates!! I add my vote to the others who would love to be able to type in a date and date range!

Thanks!

posts.author: Cait (Caitlyn)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be great to be able to copy and paste in dates the same way that you can with task text. We are finding it difficult to do the initial set up of large timelines.

posts.author: TMacc (Terri Macchion)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like this feature too. We use a column for date of birth and I’ve had to make it a simple text column to avoid scrolling back all the years.

posts.author: russadk (Russ)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please allow typing of a date in a date column. If you are typing historical dates, it’s very tedious having to click through several times to get to a previous month or year every time you enter a date. While the current feature is useful if you’re adding a date in the current month, it’s not very practical for dates outside of that. Or, allow us to create a text column that can be formatted as a date.

Thanks!

posts.author: LHebard (Laura S)
posts.cookedHtml: We have struggled with this one a little too. At times it would be nice just the manually enter the date.

posts.author: dmckearan (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree with this. The manual clicking of the dates on a date or timeline column is tedious, especially when deadlines are weeks or months away.

posts.author: mfjramirezf (Marcos Ramírez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Plus one!

Please allow us to type dates!

Just think about a list of people with date of birth… poor mouse!

posts.author: AKBurczyk (Amanda Burczyk)
posts.cookedHtml:
For two full years this has been the way?!
So horribly inefficient!
PLEASE Monday!
Allow us to enter our dates! At least let us select year and month instead of scrolling through each month.

posts.author: AmandaW (Amanda Wood)
posts.cookedHtml: YES, YES, YES!!! This would save me soooooo much time, as the expiry dates we apply to some of our Boards span a 10 year period

posts.author: ebannister (Elliot Bannister)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my vote to this! As we are using the boards for historical research, we are entering dates like 1936 which require a lot of scrolling back on the date picker. Would be much easier to type them in (like you can on Airtable… just sayin’ )

posts.author: cberman (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this! There is no reason it should take like 30 clicks to enter a single date. Click the Month/Year to open the list of years, scroll over to the group containing the year, click the year. Click the side arrows to scroll over to the month. Click the day. It is super tedious for something that is to totally fundamental to a task tracking and project tracking app, everything is linked to dates.

posts.author: JodyOdel (Jody Odel)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes!!! It would also be nice to have the option for only month/year. Currently I just use the first of each month but scrolling through dates and years is definitely a hassle. I am currently going through an entire board from a sales rep to change dates because the info pulled the year 2001 for every entry. It his entire year of sales / quotes! It is a lot!

posts.author: Keira (Keira)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree - I receive updated lists of expected delivery dates for large numbers of items. There seems to be no way to just copy and paste these into a column and without changing the field - I can’t even type, I have to select via calendar (which is very frustrating if the item is not due in for many months). This makes it very time consuming for a job which would take literally 2 seconds in Excel. (To be clear, I don’t want to import the entire spreadsheet to a new board, I just want to be able copy a single column of information to an existing board).

posts.author: AKBurczyk (Amanda Burczyk)
posts.cookedHtml: Yikes. I feel this. I recently had to enter the sales records of multiple people… multiple years. With 2 dates per record. I even went straight to support and requested this lol.

posts.author: Hello_NYC (J M)
posts.cookedHtml: Itemizing dated items with this calendar is torture!!! Really, this is insane! There 100% needs to be an option to type in a date without this calendar selection!!!

posts.author: Katy27 (Katy)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree! This should be a feature!

posts.author: CJEStaff (Jodi Portnoy)
posts.cookedHtml: This is so true and such a pain for any date more than about a month out. Monday team, any update on whether this can be added to the roadmap?

posts.author: erika (Erika Barse)
posts.cookedHtml: Please please please!

posts.author: Michael_H (Michael_H)
posts.cookedHtml: I have sent this as feedback prior joining the community here. Got my upvote, it was (and is) painful having to add loads of (very) old dates with the date picker.

posts.author: LaunchMe (Shawna Culp)
posts.cookedHtml: I need to reduce the number of clicks it takes to enter in a date in a date type column so that I can quickly add numbers instead of click/scrolling and move on to my next tasks with less finger strain more quickly.

posts.author: cas37 (CS)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s shocking this isn’t standard. It is extremely tedious to manually choose dates for every task. Please give us the option to type and copy-paste dates in.

posts.author: tapethis (Matt Tape)
posts.cookedHtml: We need to be able to type, as well as copy/paste into a date column. Copy and past cells from excel anywhere in a board should also work. It works from excel > google, and google > excel… so it shouldn’t be too difficult.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @tapethis,
You can actually do this with the Toolkit app.
Just copy from which ever source and the app will paste it directly into your chosen column. You can even select which item to start pasting from.

Also, in answer to everyone, you can use the app to type the desired date and paste it into monday.com.

If you need any help, you can always write to us at support@kolaai.com

posts.author: mary.gunn (Mary Gunn)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this on the roadmap yet? This is such a point of pain for people. <3

posts.author: kalieghyohnka (Kaliegh Yohnka)
posts.cookedHtml: YES!! I used Monday.com for HR and having to enter a date for someone’s DOB in 1955 is ridiculous

posts.author: scarletb (Scarlet)
posts.cookedHtml: This is such a basic usability requirement. Typing dates is many times faster than clicking through a calendar!

posts.author: rowan (Rowan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Adding another vote in favor of this. There are a number of things where it would be helpful to use the timeline feature to track things that took place over a long period of time. Every single time I try to enter a timeframe like this, it will take multiple attempts of jumping back and forth between the start and end date to get it right. Usually what happens is that when I click the second date, it overrides the first date I selected and I need to hunt down that date again. Especially when I’m manually entering dates that I don’t have memorized from an external document the system tends to get mad if I shift tabs to remind myself what dates I’m entering.

Typing a date is allowed for single date fields in forms, but because forms cannot update existing items that would only help in initial task creation.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml: You can actually use forms to update existing items if you use the Duplicates and Uniques app. Here is an example

posts.author: erika (Erika Barse)
posts.cookedHtml: YES! Please, it’s highly inefficient to have to scroll to select a date every time. (In addition to this, if something is in calendar form, it would be nice to be able to make the calendar sun-sat if you are not accustomed to a mon-sun layout)

posts.author: maxtownsend (Max Townsend)
posts.cookedHtml: +1, Please please please please create a way to manually type in the date vs. use the picker. I do title research and I often have to use very old dates. Also, when I export to Excel, there’s a glitch were Excel recognizes dates from 1900> but nothing older than 1900. I realize this doesn’t affect a lot of people but it would be great if it could be figured out why a date in the 1800’s can’t be recognized after export to Excel.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for taking the time to share your suggestion. Great minds think alike – our development team is already working on the capability to manually enter the date on the date picker.

Requests like yours help guide us in prioritizing the features that our customers care about the most. So keep the ideas coming because your feedback impacts tens of thousands monday.com customers who will enjoy entering dates on the date picker because of you.

Have another idea? Create a new topic in our “submit an idea” category!

posts.author: Phils (Philippe)
posts.cookedHtml: We use the date column a lot. For example, at HR we need to put in the birthday. It’s much faster to type in the date then selecting it via the date picker. Users are now always obliged to use the date picker which in this case is unhandy. You can imagine if you need to fill multiple date records like 02-04-1969 this is much slower via date picker. So it would be nice that in addition or as a replacement users could key entry a date.

posts.author: mfjramirezf (Marcos Ramírez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Phills! You can vote here

https://community.monday.com/t/allow-typing-of-date-in-date-column/18707

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for taking the time to share your suggestions. Great minds think alike – our development team is already working on the capability to manually enter the date on the date picker.

Requests like yours help guide us in prioritizing the features that our customers care about the most. So keep the ideas coming because your feedback impacts tens of thousands monday.com customers who will enjoy entering dates on the date picker because of you.

posts.author: only1here (John)
posts.cookedHtml:
There are occasions where manually typing a date into a date column is much quicker than clicking on the field and searching through the months to find the correct date.

I’m implementing a document register / review board and some of the effective dates are in the past, in some cases a couple of years ago. I did import my existing register from excel to monday.com but I didn’t notice that some of the data was not matching. It was too late to start again.

I now need to manually go through and amend a few fields. It would be quicker to manually type in the date using the keyboard. What is everyone’s thoughts o this?

posts.author: VasylTretiakov (Vasyl Tretiakov)
posts.cookedHtml: I fully agree. Inputting birthdays seems faster by typing from keyboard then clicking through years, then months and dates.

posts.author: evanovich (Evan)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is absolutely mind boggling to me that a user can’t set up a new board using solely the keyboard. I cannot be alone in this sentiment.

My use case/ask: I’m setting up a new board based on a project schedule and after I have my columns set up, I would like to click once in the top left project cell and type my input, tab, hit spc/enter to activate the next for the menu, short cut keys or use arrow keys to selection, enter or tab to next field and so on. This is especially frustrating when it comes to DATES. Like when entering a series of deadlines we should absolutely have the ability to mouse our way to the date or, in my case, when my deadline is more than a year out, be able to type the date in MMDDYY (localization appropriate) format and keep on rolling.

posts.author: emmarosie2607 (emmarosie2607)
posts.cookedHtml: Well, I think the easiest way is typing manually a date into a date column using keyboard than clicking sections of years, months and dates. This process is easy and quick. But sometimes we don’t have the option to write date manually from keyboard. Then we have to go through a long process.

posts.author: Gal5252 (Gal)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree 100%. That bothers me as well and consumes time.

posts.author: vietminhk14 (Viet Do)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday team,

I feel it is going back to 2000 when I need to go through the whole calendar to pick up the DOB of the customer.
It would be great if we are able to enter or copy date into date column with specific date format which will work for you either DDMMYYYY or YYYYMMDD.
I am feeling exhausted to import 50k customers history into Monday.com and losing customers’ DOB.

Thank you,
Viet

posts.author: gg37 (Gary Garrett)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, is there any update on this? It seems like this has been in discussion for about 3 years now, with a large amount of people seeing this update as essential? When will this update be implemented. Many other programs allow typing of dates so it should not be a difficult add.

posts.author: vietminhk14 (Viet Do)
posts.cookedHtml:
This post started since Feb 2020, and nothing updated yet!
It seems like Monday team doesn’t hear us well!

posts.author: russadk (Russ)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please allow typing of a date in a date column. If you are typing historical dates, it’s very tedious having to click through several times to get to a previous month or year every time you enter a date. While the current feature is useful if you’re adding a date in the current month, it’s not very practical for dates outside of that. Or, allow us to create a text column that can be formatted as a date.

Thanks!

posts.author: frontiertax (Tina)
posts.cookedHtml: Why is not not possible to TYPE in the date instead of having to pick from a calendar? It’s very tedious to click all the way to December when we are presently in January, especially when I have a bunch of tasks I need to set due dates on. I can’t really use formula because we are an accounting firm and there are many different types of due dates. But I would HIGHLY suggest to be able to TYPE in the date instead of picking it form a calendar, it’s going to save a lot of time!

posts.author: lrgroom (Lisa Groom)
posts.cookedHtml:
When there is a date field you currently have to select the forward/backward arrows to change the month. Add drop down to change the Month like you do for the year for fewer clicks.
Also would be nice if you could just type in the date vs. being forced to use the pop up.

posts.author: AlexTechnical (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey, any traction on this? I’d love to be able to just type, for example, 5.3.23 in a field rather than clicking and scrolling. TBH I’m thinking of just switching to a text field and losing the date functionality because it would be less trouble.

posts.author: davidboag (David Alexander Boag)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1,000

Not being able to quickly enter a date (or paste it from another app) is needlessly time-consuming.

posts.author: joris.kempen (Joris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Really insane that have been working for 2 years on such a basic feature…

Having to do 300 manual date selections, time difference is at least 5-10 seconds…

posts.author: maxtownsend (Max Townsend)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday.com - How far out is the fix / feature?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Everyone, thank you for your feedback and part in helping us develop this!

The product team is excited to involve the community in our development and release process. Check out the latest update here.

image686×720 57.6 KB

posts.author: SeanM (Sean)
posts.cookedHtml: When is this available for customers? you posted this 25 days ago and i still dont see this option on our Monday environment…

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Community!

Excited to share this this is now being gradually released and you should see this feature on your board shortly! Thank you for all your feedback and joining us in the development process.

Hope you enjoying using the new date picker!

posts.author: ZGC (Zoé Guiraudon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, what about the period column though? Can we have date pickers for that one too? It is especially annoying when we deal with long period of time

posts.author: susiecreamcheese (Susan)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi. I’m just trying out Monday to see if it’s worth switching from Asana, but this one issue of not being to type the date is NOT functional for me on the intro Pro plan. I confess, this is turning out to be the most detrimental thing to us switching ffrom Asana to Monday. Please tell me what I have to do to make this work as evryone requested thru out 2023 (now in Fall 2024… I don’t see the problem resolved. Plz shed liight. )

posts.author: Flaviomian (Flavio Mian)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, I’d like just a minor change to this feature.

When entering the date, with keyboard, the standard order is Month-Day-Year (i.e. 01-23-2025)

It would be great to change this order based on the country standards. For example, in Brazil, we enter Day-Month-Year.
